plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and align with conservative limits.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% for more income and broad index exposure.
Buy XLF at 5% to diversify into a strong financial sector.
Add PANW at 5% for high-quality software exposure.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and reduces overall portfolio risk.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest ~1.5% monthly return by de-risking significantly. Expect lower volatility and drawdowns, but also limited upside potential. The focus is on capital preservation and stability.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits.
Sell SOXL entirely due to its 'below stop' flag.
Maintain a small 2% position in SPAL for limited leveraged exposure.
Increase CRWD to 18% to press a winning position.
Buy PANW at 8% and MSFT at 8% to add high-quality tech setups.
This plan balances growth potential with risk management.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for ~2.5% monthly growth by managing risk and leveraging quality tech. It seeks a blend of broad market exposure and growth, with moderate volatility. Drawdowns are expected to be contained but present.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with concentration limits.
Sell SOXL entirely as it is below its stop.
Increase CRWD to 25% to maximize exposure to a strong uptrend.
Buy PANW at 10% and NVDU at 8% for high-conviction tech and chip exposure.
Add ASML at 8% to further concentrate in strong semiconductor names.
This plan aggressively leans into high-scoring setups and allows for higher concentration.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% monthly growth by concentrating in high-scoring setups and using leverage. Be aware of significantly higher volatility and potential for larger drawdowns. Leveraged ETFs like SOXL (though sold here) and other leveraged positions can experience decay and amplify losses quickly; strict stop discipline is critical.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and free up capital.
Increase QQQI to 35% to maximize income generation.
Reduce QQQ to 10% and CRWD to 5% to lower growth and high-beta exposure.
Trim SMH to 5% to reduce semiconductor concentration.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk.
This plan prioritizes steady distributions and capital preservation over growth.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1% monthly distributions by rotating into income-focused ETFs. While aiming for stability and regular payouts, distributions are variable and NAV can still decline in market downturns. This is not a savings account; expect minimal capital growth and low drawdown potential.
